1	"""Multi-agent orchestrator demo.
     2	
     3	Run from the **repository root** so paths like ``./test_demo/skills/...`` resolve correctly.
     4	
     5	Setup::
     6	    uv sync --extra demo
     7	
     8	    python -m test_demo.orchestrator                    # streaming chat (default)
     9	    python -m test_demo.orchestrator --chat             # same: stream assistant tokens
    10	    python -m test_demo.orchestrator --chat_sync        # chat without token streaming
    11	    python -m test_demo.orchestrator --chat --session <id>     # resume (``--chat_sync`` also ok)
    12	
    13	**Resume:** pass **``--session <id>``** together with **``--chat``** or **``--chat_sync``**.
    14	
    15	In the REPL: **Enter** sends the message; **/bye** exits and prints how to resume with the same id.
    16	
    17	Installing the ``deepx`` distribution exposes ``deepx`` and ``deepx_cli``; this orchestrator
    18	lives under ``test_demo`` for deepx demonstration purposes only.
    19	"""
    20	
    21	from __future__ import annotations
    22	
    23	import argparse
    24	import mimetypes
    25	import sys
    26	from pathlib import Path
    27	
    28	REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
    29	if str(REPO_ROOT) not in sys.path:
    30	    sys.path.insert(0, str(REPO_ROOT))
    31	
    32	from dotenv import load_dotenv  # noqa: E402
    33	
    34	load_dotenv()
    35	
    36	from agents import RunContextWrapper, function_tool  # noqa: E402
    37	from rich.console import Console  # noqa: E402
    38	from rich.panel import Panel  # noqa: E402
    39	from rich.text import Text  # noqa: E402
    40	
    41	from deepx import create_deep_agent  # noqa: E402
    42	from deepx.backends.filesystem import FilesystemBackend  # noqa: E402
    43	from deepx.backends.protocol import BackendProtocol  # noqa: E402
    44	from deepx.context import AgentContext  # noqa: E402
    45	from test_demo.hf_agent import hf_agent_runner  # noqa: E402
    46	from test_demo.pdf_agent import pdf_agent_runner  # noqa: E402
    47	from test_demo.sql_agent import sql_agent_runner  # noqa: E402
    48	from test_demo.web_agent import web_agent_runner  # noqa: E402
    49	
    50	_PREVIEW_HEAD_LINES = 70
    51	_PREVIEW_TAIL_LINES = 35
    52	_MAX_PDF_PAGES_PREVIEW = 2
    53	_render_console = Console(highlight=False)
    54	
    55	
    56	def _line_preview_body(text: str, *, head: int, tail: int) -> str:
    57	    lines = text.splitlines()
    58	    n = len(lines)
    59	    if n <= head + tail:
    60	        return text
    61	    mid_omitted = n - head - tail
    62	    return (
    63	        "\n".join(lines[:head])
    64	        + f"\n\n… ({mid_omitted} lines omitted) …\n\n"
    65	        + "\n".join(lines[-tail:])
    66	    )
    67	
    68	
    69	def _preview_plain_file(backend: BackendProtocol, sid: str, agent_path: str) -> str:
    70	    rr = backend.read(sid, agent_path, 0, _PREVIEW_HEAD_LINES)
    71	    if rr.error:
    72	        return rr.error
    73	    total = rr.total_lines
    74	    head = rr.content or ""
    75	    if total is None:
    76	        return _line_preview_body(
    77	            head, head=_PREVIEW_HEAD_LINES, tail=_PREVIEW_TAIL_LINES
    78	        )
    79	    if total <= _PREVIEW_HEAD_LINES:
    80	        return head
    81	    tail_start = max(_PREVIEW_HEAD_LINES, total - _PREVIEW_TAIL_LINES)
    82	    rr2 = backend.read(sid, agent_path, tail_start, total - tail_start)
    83	    tail = rr2.content or ""
    84	    omitted = tail_start - _PREVIEW_HEAD_LINES
    85	    if omitted > 0:
    86	        return head + f"\n\n… ({omitted} lines omitted) …\n\n" + tail
    87	    return head + "\n\n" + tail
    88	
    89	
    90	def render_pdf_as_text(
    91	    ctx: RunContextWrapper[AgentContext], sid: str, agent_path: str
    92	) -> str:
    93	    """Extract text from a PDF using pdfplumber (bounded for terminal display)."""
    94	    try:
    95	        import pdfplumber
    96	    except ImportError:
    97	        return "[pdfplumber not installed — cannot render PDF as text]"
    98	
    99	    resolved = ctx.context.backend.resolve_path(sid, agent_path)
   100	    if resolved is None or not Path(resolved).exists():
   101	        return f"File not found: {agent_path}"
   102	
   103	    try:
   104	        pages: list[str] = []
   105	        with pdfplumber.open(resolved) as pdf:
   106	            n_pages = len(pdf.pages)
   107	            for i, page in enumerate(pdf.pages, start=1):
   108	                if i > _MAX_PDF_PAGES_PREVIEW:
   109	                    rest = n_pages - _MAX_PDF_PAGES_PREVIEW
   110	                    pages.append(f"… ({rest} more pages omitted from preview) …")
   111	                    break
   112	                text = (page.extract_text() or "").strip()
   113	                if text:
   114	                    pages.append(f"── Page {i} ──\n{text}")
   115	
   116	        if not pages:
   117	            return "(PDF has no extractable text — may be scanned/image-only)"
   118	
   119	        full = "\n\n".join(pages)
   120	        return _line_preview_body(
   121	            full, head=_PREVIEW_HEAD_LINES, tail=_PREVIEW_TAIL_LINES
   122	        )
   123	
   124	    except Exception as exc:
   125	        return f"PDF render error: {exc}"
   126	
   127	
   128	@function_tool
   129	async def render_files(
   130	    ctx: RunContextWrapper[AgentContext],
   131	    paths: list[str],
   132	) -> str:
   133	    """Show finished file contents to the human in the terminal (Rich panels).
   134	
   135	    The user sees the **full** rendered content for each path in their terminal. Call **once**
   136	    when work is **complete** (or when they should see final outputs), with **every** relevant path.
   137	    Do **not** use this for exploratory reading while delegating.
   138	
   139	    After this returns, **do not** tell the user you "rendered" or "displayed" the file, **do not**
   140	    repeat the file body or long excerpts in chat, and **do not** offer to paste or open the same
   141	    paths unless the user asks — they already saw it in the panel.
   142	    """
   143	    sid = ctx.context.session_id
   144	    parts: list[str] = []
   145	    w = _render_console.size.width or 120
   146	
   147	    for path in paths:
   148	        p = (path or "").strip()
   149	        if not p:
   150	            continue
   151	
   152	        mime, _ = mimetypes.guess_type(p)
   153	        is_pdf = (mime == "application/pdf") or p.lower().endswith(".pdf")
   154	
   155	        if is_pdf:
   156	            panel_content = render_pdf_as_text(ctx, sid, p)
   157	        else:
   158	            panel_content = _preview_plain_file(ctx.context.backend, sid, p)
   159	
   160	        _render_console.print(
   161	            Panel(
   162	                Text(panel_content),
   163	                title=Text(f"render_files · {p}"),
   164	                border_style="yellow",
   165	                expand=True,
   166	                width=w,
   167	            )
   168	        )
   169	        parts.append(p)
   170	
   171	    return "Rendered: " + "; ".join(parts) if parts else "No paths provided."
   172	
   173	
   174	DBS_DIR = REPO_ROOT / "test_demo" / "dbs"
   175	TEST_DBS = DBS_DIR / "test_dbs"
   176	AGENT_DBS = DBS_DIR / "agent_dbs"
   177	for d in (TEST_DBS, AGENT_DBS):
   178	    d.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   179	
   180	DEMO_BACKEND = FilesystemBackend(REPO_ROOT)
   181	
   182	ORCH_DB = str(AGENT_DBS / "orchestrator.db")
   183	
   184	orch_tools = [render_files]
   185	
   186	DEMO_ORCHESTRATOR_SYS = """\
   187	**Coordinate specialists when the task matches the routing table below.** For those tasks you do **not**
   188	run web/Tavily, SQL, PDF, or HF work yourself — you delegate with a **single, self-contained brief**
   189	(including output paths, `db_name` when needed, and reasonable defaults like “top 3 with ties” if the user
   190	already implied them).
   191	
   192	**General work (no matching specialist):** answer from context, use your file tools, or guide the user.
   193	Examples: skill/marketplace setup, LobeHub install steps, chit-chat, or anything that is **not** purely
   194	web research, SQL on demo DBs, PDF workflows, or HF Hub MCP. This demo orchestrator has **no** host `execute`
   195	tool — you cannot run shell commands yourself; use `read_file` / planning tools and clear instructions, or
   196	route web execution to **`web_agent`** when the user needs live fetching.
   197	
   198	**Delegate first; almost never ask the user to “confirm” the task shape.** If the user already stated
   199	constraints (e.g. “top three genres and top three artists”), **do not** re-ask or paraphrase into clarifying
   200	questions — pick sound defaults, state them in the brief, and call the specialist. Ask the user **only** when
   201	something **objectively required** is missing (e.g. which `*.db` when several apply and the user gave none).
   202	
   203	**Do not overcomplicate the task**. Limit the response to the smallest useful action that directly addresses the user’s request. Avoid expanding into broader analysis, multiple steps, or extra context unless the user asks for it.
   204	
   205	**Specialist outputs:** In every delegation brief, tell the specialist to **write deliverables to files**
   206	under **`/_outputs/`** (exact paths you agree on, e.g. `/_outputs/diffusion_models_summary.md`) and to
   207	return **only paths plus a short summary** — not full file bodies.
   208	
   209	**After a specialist tool returns:** trust its **paths** and **short summary**. Do **not** `read_file` those
   210	files. To return those outputs to the user, call **`render_files`** once with **all** final paths.
   211	
   212	**After `render_files`:** the user has seen the **full** files in the terminal. Reply with a **compact**
   213	shortlist, **paths**, and **recommendation** only — **no** “I showed you the file”, **no** repeating the
   214	digest, **no** offering to paste or open the same paths unless the user explicitly asks.
   215	
   216	**If a specialist’s reply contains a question for the user:** relay it briefly; do not stack your own
   217	extra questions on top.
   218	
   219	## Routing
   220	
   221	| Goal | Use |
   222	|------|-----|
   223	| Live web research, citations, long-form markdown from the open web | **`web_agent` tool** — one self-contained brief; specialist uses `tvly`, tavily skills, and **write-report** for deliverables. |
   224	| Read-only SQL on sample ``*.db`` files under `test_demo/dbs/test_dbs` | **`sql_agent` tool** — self-contained brief; every query needs **`db_name`** (e.g. `chinook.db`). |
   225	| PDF workflows | **`pdf_agent` tool**. |
   226	| Hugging Face Hub | **`hf_agent` tool** when configured (**HF_TOKEN**); keep delegation brief. |
   227	| Show finished text to the user | **`render_files`** once, with every relevant path — not for mid-task browsing. |
   228	
   229	**Delegation:** one strong **`tool` call per specialist** when possible; pass **paths** between
   230	steps, not pasted bodies.
   231	"""
   232	
   233	_ORCH_SUBAGENTS = [
   234	    web_agent_runner,
   235	    sql_agent_runner,
   236	    pdf_agent_runner,
   237	]
   238	if hf_agent_runner is not None:
   239	    _ORCH_SUBAGENTS.append(hf_agent_runner)
   240	
   241	
   242	orchestrator_runner = create_deep_agent(
   243	    name="orchestrator",
   244	    description=(
   245	        "Coordinates **web_agent** (Tavily CLI + report standards), **sql_agent**, **pdf_agent**, and "
   246	        "optional **hf_agent**. Delegates with self-contained briefs; specialists write under **/_outputs/**. "
   247	        "You plan, route, and use **render_files**—you do not replace specialists for their domains."
   248	    ),
   249	    subagents=_ORCH_SUBAGENTS,
   250	    memory=[".deepx/AGENTS.md"],
   251	    tools=orch_tools,
   252	    skills=[],
   253	    system_prompt=DEMO_ORCHESTRATOR_SYS,
   254	    checkpointer=ORCH_DB,
   255	    debug=True,
   256	    backend=DEMO_BACKEND,
   257	)
   258	
   259	
   260	def main() -> None:
   261	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Deepx orchestrator demo (terminal).")
   262	    parser.add_argument(
   263	        "--session",
   264	        default=None,
   265	        metavar="ID",
   266	        help="Resume this session id (must be used with --chat or --chat_sync).",
   267	    )
   268	    parser.add_argument(
   269	        "--chat",
   270	        action="store_true",
   271	        help="Interactive multi-turn session with streaming (default if --chat_sync not set).",
   272	    )
   273	    parser.add_argument(
   274	        "--chat_sync",
   275	        action="store_true",
   276	        help="Interactive multi-turn session without token streaming.",
   277	    )
   278	    args, _rest = parser.parse_known_args()
   279	
   280	    if args.session is not None and not args.chat and not args.chat_sync:
   281	        parser.error("--session requires --chat or --chat_sync")
   282	
   283	    from deepx_cli.chat_stream import run_chat_stream
   284	    from deepx_cli.chat_sync import run_chat_sync
   285	
   286	    if not args.chat_sync:
   287	        run_chat_stream(orchestrator_runner)
   288	    else:
   289	        run_chat_sync(orchestrator_runner)
   290	
   291	
   292	if __name__ == "__main__":
   293	    main()
